history.name: Foundation Work
history.impact: The start and end dates for Foundation Work are incorrectly set, with the end date preceding the start date. This error in scheduling directly impacts the sequence and timing of the Foundation Work, necessitating immediate correction to align with the project plan.

name: Foundation Work
description: Excavating, installing footings, and pouring the foundation.

history.name: Foundation Work
history.impact: Correction of dates is required to prevent miscommunication and potential delays in the project schedule. No direct dependencies listed, but clarity in scheduling is essential.

name: Removal of slabs, foundation and site improvements (560 semi dump loads)
description: removal of slabs, foundation and site improvement, mostly hauling activity

history.name: Removal of slabs, foundation and site improvements (560 semi dump loads)
history.impact: Dependent on 'SuperStructure clean up parking structure' and 'Superstructure Demolition of Main Structure'. This dependency chain is critical for the Site Completion and requires close monitoring to avoid delays.

name: Demolition of structure behind main building

history.name: Demolition of structure behind main building
history.impact: Depends on 'Superstructure Demolition of Main Structure', which in turn depends on 'SuperStructure clean up parking structure'. Delays in the cleanup can cause cascading delays in this and subsequent activities.

history.name: Superstructure Demolition of Main Structure
history.impact: This critical activity's delay can impact the schedule significantly, especially for activities like 'Demolition of structure behind main building'. Close monitoring and additional resources might be required to maintain schedule.

history.name: Site Completion
history.impact: Dependent on the completion of 'Removal of slabs, foundation and site improvements (560 semi dump loads)', which depends on other key activities. This is a critical milestone that affects several subsequent activities, requiring frequent progress reviews to mitigate delays.

history.name: Site Completion
history.impact: Dependent on the completion of 'Removal of slabs, foundation and site improvements (560 semi dump loads)', which depends on other key activities. This is a critical milestone that affects several subsequent activities, requiring frequent progress reviews to mitigate delays.

name: Cap Sewer
description: Cap sewer, make sure permits are in place

history.name: Cap Sewer
history.impact: A delay in the 'Removal of slabs, foundation and site improvements' activity extends the start date, impacting 'Punch List items' which is dependent on 'Cap Sewer'.
history.message:
Whats the image about ?
attached image description - The image shows a presentation slide titled "Cost QRA Results" being displayed during a lecture or seminar. The slide is about the pre-mitigated cost Quantitative Risk Analysis (QRA) results, which contribute to a recommended contingency for a project. 

The table on the slide lists different components of costs under three different confidence levels (1% CL, 80% CL, and 99% CL). Various cost components include Construction Cost of Risk, Design Cost of Risk, Cost of Schedule Delay, CEU (no further description provided), and Recommended Contingency. Each component's cost is shown at different confidence levels.

A histogram chart titled Figure 10 shows the Distribution for Cost of Schedule Delay, marking the intervals between 1% CL and 80% CL. This chart visually represents the probability distribution of schedule delay costs.

At the bottom of the slide, there's a URL that seems to link to additional resources or the homepage of the organization giving the talk. The image also captures part of the room with a projector screen, a visible arm of a person presumably the presenter, a wooden bench, and an old-fashioned radiator in the background.

history.name: Site Completion
history.impact: Directly impacted by the 'Removal of slabs, foundation and site improvements' activity's delay, leading to a ripple effect on 'Demobilization of Equipment', 'Permit Sign offs', and 'Repairs/Modifications to Temp. Protection and SWPPP Measures'.
